title: Tracking: SVA roadmap and progress
body:
## SVA: Support roadmap and progress tracking

Tracking issue for incremental SVA support improvements.  Last updated: 2026-04-09.

### Open Issues

| Issue | Feature | Status |
|---|---|---|
| ~~#4017~~ | ~~`##` delay at start of property definition~~ | ✅ #7298, #7312, #7377 |
| #4038 | Controlling vacuous success | Open |
| ~~#4263~~ | ~~`##0` cycle delays~~ | ✅ #7298 |
| ~~#4425~~ | ~~Named `sequence` declarations~~ | ✅ #7283 |
| #4848 | `disable iff` in complex scenarios | Open |
| ~~#5979~~ | ~~Property-local variable declarations~~ | ✅ #7286 |
| #7290 | `until` property | 🔄 @RRozak |
| #7291 | `s_eventually` property | 🔄 @b-chmiel |
| #7292 | `\|=>` implication IEEE compliance | 🔄 @b-chmiel |

### Unsupported Features

67 unique UNSUPPORTED messages across the codebase. Grouped below as candidate tasks.

#### Part 1: Sequence Foundation — ✅ Complete

| Feature | IEEE | Status | PR |
|---|---|---|---|
| Named sequence reference | 16.8 | ✅ | #7283 |
| Sequence `and` / `or` | 16.9 | ✅ | #7285 |
| Sequence match items | 16.11 | ✅ | #7286 |
| Property-local variables | 16.10 | ✅ | #7286 |
| Implication with sequence expr (RHS) | 16.12 | ✅ | #7284 |

#### Part 2: Delay and Repetition — ✅ Complete

| Feature | IEEE | Status | PR |
|---|---|---|---|
| `##0` synchronous delay | 16.7 | ✅ | #7298 |
| `##[M:N]` range delay | 16.7 | ✅ | #7312 |
| `##[*]` / `##[+]` / `##[M:$]` unbounded delay | 16.7 | ✅ | #7377 |
| `[*N]` consecutive repetition | 16.9.2 | ✅ | #7311 |
| `[*N:M]` / `[*]` range and unbounded repetition | 16.9.2 | ✅ | #7379 |
| `[+]` one-or-more repetition | 16.9.2 | ✅ | #7379 |
| `[->N]` goto repetition | 16.9.2 | ✅ | #7310 |
| `[=N]` non-consecutive repetition | 16.9.2 | | |
| `first_match` | 16.9 | | |
| Boolean abbreviation | 16.9.2 | | |

#### Part 3: Sequence Combinators — In Progress

| Feature | IEEE | Status | PR |
|---|---|---|---|
| `intersect` | 16.9 | ✅ | #7374 |
| `throughout` | 16.9 | ✅ | #7378 |
| `within` | 16.9 | | |

#### Part 4: Temporal Property Operators — In Progress

| Feature | IEEE | Status | PR/Issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| `always` / `always[]` | 16.12 | | |
| `s_always` | 16.12 | | |
| `eventually[]` / `s_eventually` / `s_eventually[]` | 16.12 | 🔄 | #7291 (@b-chmiel) |
| `nexttime` / `nexttime[]` / `s_nexttime` / `s_nexttime[]` | 16.12 | 🔄 | #7364 (@toddstrader) |
| `until` / `s_until` / `until_with` / `s_until_with` | 16.12 | 🔄 | #7290 (@RRozak) |
| `strong` / `weak` | 16.12 | | |

#### Part 5: Property Abort and Connectives

| Feature | IEEE | Status | PR |
|---|---|---|---|
| `accept_on` / `reject_on` | 16.12 | | |
| `sync_accept_on` / `sync_reject_on` | 16.12 | | |
| `#-#` non-overlapping followed-by | 16.12 | | |
| `#=#` overlapping followed-by | 16.12 | | |

#### Part 6: Declarations, Context, and Integration

| Feature | IEEE | Status | PR |
|---|---|---|---|
| Property/sequence argument data types | 16.8, 16.12 | | |
| Property case/if expression | 16.12 | | |
| Property local-port qualifier | 16.8, 16.12 | | |
| `disable iff` before `@` ordering | 16.15 | | |
| Dual-clock property calls | 16.13 | | |
| Procedural concurrent assertions | 16.14 | | |
| Unclocked assertions | 16.16 | | |
| Recursive property calls | 16.12 | | |
| `$sampled` restrictions | 20.12 | | |
| Future value restrictions | 20.12 | | |

#### Part 7: Assert Control

| Feature | IEEE | Status | PR |
|---|---|---|---|
| Control types | 20.11 | | |
| Assertion type filtering | 20.11 | | |
| Non-const control expressions | 20.11 | | |
| Assertcontrols in classes | 20.11 | | |

### Already Supported (by merged PRs)

- Immediate assertions: `assert`, `assume`, `cover` (#2531)
- `|->` and `|=>` implication (#2534)
- `not` property (#3572)
- Named properties (#3667)
- Multi-expression sequences `a ##1 b ##2 c` (#6639, #6508, #6427)
- Compile-time constant cycle delays (#6691)
- `disable iff` in properties and sequences (#6783, #6890, #7090, #7337)
- `eventually[]` parsing (#6530)
- Pre/post increments in assertions (#6434)
- `--assert-case` option (#4919)
- Assertions in `final` blocks (#7249)
- Value sampling: `$past`, `$rose`, `$fell`, `$stable`, `$sampled` (#3569, #5931, #6652, #6689, #7093, #7386)
- Clocking blocks (#3674, #5184, #5235, #5430, #6436, #6788)
- Named sequence declarations and instances (#7283)
- `##` delay on implication RHS (#7284)
- Boolean `and`/`or` in sequence expressions (#7285)
- Property-local variables and sequence match items (#7286)
- `##0` cycle delays (#7298)
- `##[M:N]` range cycle delay (#7312)
- `[*N]` / `[*N:M]` / `[*]` / `[+]` consecutive repetition (#7311, #7379)
- `[->N]` goto repetition (#7310)
- `##[*]` / `##[+]` / `##[M:$]` unbounded cycle delays (#7377)
- `intersect` sequence operator (#7374)
- `throughout` sequence operator (#7378)

### Notes from discusssion #7254 

- Incremental PRs preferred over monolithic drops
- Performance is a hard constraint -- assertions can be 20%+ of runtime in large designs
- [YosysHQ/SymbiYosys](https://github.com/YosysHQ/SymbiYosys) tests may provide additional coverage
- Part ordering is a guideline -- individual features can land independently

---
Maintained by PlanV, updated as features land, assisted with Claude Code.
Reviewed by Yilou Wang.
